# synix

> A build system for agent memory — declarative pipelines transform raw conversations into searchable, hierarchical memory with full provenance tracking

## What it does

Synix processes conversation exports (ChatGPT, Claude, text files) through configurable DAG pipelines of LLM transforms to build memory artifacts at multiple levels of abstraction. Think `make` or `dbt` for agent memory: declare your architecture in Python, build incrementally with content-addressed caching, trace any artifact back to source conversations.

## Key concepts

- **Artifact** — immutable, versioned build output with SHA256 content hash and provenance chain
- **Layer** — typed pipeline step (Source/Transform/SearchIndex/FlatFile) with dependency references  
- **Pipeline** — Python object containing layers, projections, and config; routes data through DAG
- **Transform** — 1:1 LLM step (episode summary, synthesis); extensible with custom classes
- **Projection** — materializes artifacts into usable outputs (SQLite FTS5 search, markdown context)
- **Provenance** — every artifact traces back through dependency graph to source conversations
- **Fingerprint** — content-addressed cache key capturing inputs, prompts, model config, transform code

## Installation and quick start

```bash
# Install
uvx synix --help

# Initialize project with sample data
uvx synix init my-project
cd my-project

# Build the pipeline (requires LLM API key in config)
uvx synix build

# Search and explore
uvx synix search "hiking"
uvx synix list
uvx synix show final-report
```

## CLI commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `uvx synix init <name>` | Scaffold new project with pipeline and sources |
| `uvx synix build [pipeline.py]` | Run pipeline, only rebuild what changed |
| `uvx synix plan [pipeline.py]` | Dry-run showing what would build with cost estimates |
| `uvx synix search <query>` | Search artifacts with provenance, `--layers` to filter |
| `uvx synix show <id>` | Display artifact content, resolves by label or ID prefix |
| `uvx synix list [layer]` | List all artifacts, optionally filtered by layer |
| `uvx synix lineage <id>` | Show full provenance chain for artifact |
| `uvx synix validate` | *(Experimental)* Run declared validators |
| `uvx synix verify` | Check build integrity (hashes, provenance) |
| `uvx synix clean` | Delete build directory |

## Architecture overview

```
src/synix/
├── cli/                   # Click commands (build, search, validate, etc.)
├── core/
│   └── models.py          # Layer hierarchy, Pipeline, Artifact models
├── build/
│   ├── runner.py          # Execute pipeline, walk DAG, cache artifacts  
│   ├── plan.py            # Dry-run planner, rebuild/cache decisions
│   ├── dag.py             # DAG resolution, build order from dependencies
│   ├── artifacts.py       # Artifact storage (filesystem-backed)
│   ├── provenance.py      # Lineage tracking and queries
│   ├── fingerprint.py     # Build fingerprints for caching
│   ├── llm_transforms.py  # Built-in transforms (episode, rollup, synthesis)
│   └── validators.py      # Built-in validators (PII, semantic conflict)
├── search/
│   ├── indexer.py         # SQLite FTS5 + embedding index builder
│   ├── retriever.py       # Hybrid search with RRF fusion  
│   └── embeddings.py      # Embedding providers (fastembed, OpenAI)
└── transforms/            # Public API re-exports
```

## Pipeline definition

Pipelines are Python code defining the memory architecture:

```python
from synix import Pipeline, Source, SearchIndex, FlatFile
from synix.transforms import EpisodeSummary, MonthlyRollup, CoreSynthesis

pipeline = Pipeline("my-memory")
pipeline.source_dir = "./sources"
pipeline.llm_config = {"model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", "temperature": 0.3}

# Layers — the memory hierarchy
transcripts = Source("transcripts") 
episodes = EpisodeSummary("episodes", depends_on=[transcripts])
monthly = MonthlyRollup("monthly", depends_on=[episodes])  
core = CoreSynthesis("core", depends_on=[monthly])

pipeline.add(transcripts, episodes, monthly, core)

# Projections — how artifacts become usable
pipeline.add(SearchIndex("memory-index", sources=[episodes, monthly, core]))
pipeline.add(FlatFile("context-doc", sources=[core], output_path="./context.md"))
```

## Important constraints

- **SQLite only** — no external databases, filesystem + SQLite FTS5 for all storage
- **No web UI** — CLI-first tool, outputs can be consumed by any system  
- **Python 3.11+** required with UV package manager (`uv sync`, `uv run synix`)
- **LLM API key** required (Anthropic/OpenAI) for transforms, configured via pipeline
- **Experimental APIs** — validate/fix workflow APIs may change in future releases
- **Source file cleanup** — deleted source files don't auto-remove downstream artifacts
